headline: Nussmeier's efficiency tops Thomas's volume as LSU escapes Eugene
body: Garrett Nussmeier didn't need to air it out like Oregon's Thomas did. The LSU quarterback completed 16 of 29 passes for 212 yards and three touchdowns, working within the Tigers' scheme to pick apart the Oregon secondary in surgical fashion. Thomas answered with 236 yards and three scores of his own on 42 attempts, but the volume masked a persistent accuracy problem that ultimately cost the Ducks. When Oregon needed precision in the red zone late, Thomas couldn't deliver it.

The pivotal difference came down to receiver play and timing. Nussmeier found Cake repeatedly in space, and the receiver's seven catches for 117 yards and two touchdowns gave LSU a consistent vertical threat that Oregon's defense couldn't shut down. Oregon countered with Bryant's big 70-yard performance, but three catches don't provide the same rhythm or predictability that Cake's involvement created. LSU's passing attack hummed despite running just 78 yards on the ground, leaning instead on short-to-intermediate routes that moved the chains.

Oregon's defense registered three sacks through Mothudi's relentless pursuit, yet those splashy plays didn't translate into three-and-outs. LSU's offense bent but didn't break, and a ground game that totaled just 78 yards proved sufficient because Nussmeier made quick decisions and Cake was always the second read or the escape valve. The Ducks dominated yardage—355 to 250—but couldn't overcome the efficiency gap. In close games, ball security and decision-making trump volume every time.
turningPoint: With the game in reach late, Nussmeier connected with Cake for a score that pushed LSU ahead with insufficient time remaining for Oregon's passing game to generate another answer despite Thomas's arm talent.

unsungHero.name: Cake
unsungHero.why: While Nussmeier earned Player of the Game for quarterback play, Cake's seven receptions and two touchdowns were the actual targets that made those throws possible and created the decisive offensive rhythm LSU needed to win the possession battle.
